# Feature Request Example

This folder shows one complete example of the document flow for a realistic product change.

## Example Feature

Add a team invite modal that lets workspace admins:

- invite one or more teammates by email
- assign a role before sending
- resend pending invites
- handle validation and permission errors cleanly

## Document Flow

1. Start with [PRD.md](/Users/mac/Desktop/other/全栈/docs/templates/feature-request-example/PRD.md) to define scope, user value, and acceptance criteria.
2. Use [design-spec.md](/Users/mac/Desktop/other/全栈/docs/templates/feature-request-example/design-spec.md) to capture screens, states, content, and accessibility requirements.
3. Use [implementation-plan.md](/Users/mac/Desktop/other/全栈/docs/templates/feature-request-example/implementation-plan.md) to turn the approved docs into code tasks, prompts, and a test plan.
4. Use [task-breakdown.md](/Users/mac/Desktop/other/全栈/docs/templates/feature-request-example/task-breakdown.md) to split the work into reviewable execution tasks and dependencies.
5. Use [release-checklist.md](/Users/mac/Desktop/other/全栈/docs/templates/feature-request-example/release-checklist.md) to prepare deploy, monitoring, and rollback.

## Why This Example Works

This feature is small enough to understand quickly, but it still exercises the whole workflow:

- product scope and non-goals
- modal UI and component states
- backend and frontend changes
- permission handling
- analytics and monitoring
- staged rollout and rollback

## How To Reuse

- Copy the four example files into a feature-specific folder.
- Replace the feature name, dates, owners, and linked assets.
- Keep the same structure unless your repo already has a stronger standard.
